overview: TCS is one of India's largest IT services companies. The interview process typically involves aptitude tests, technical interviews, and HR rounds.

rounds.name: TCS NQT (National Qualifier Test)
rounds.description: Online aptitude test covering verbal, quantitative, and programming ability
tips: Practice verbal ability - synonyms, antonyms, reading comprehension
Focus on quantitative aptitude - percentages, ratios, time & work
Programming section tests logic and basic coding

rounds.name: Technical Interview
rounds.description: Tests core CS fundamentals and project knowledge
tips: Be thorough with OOPS concepts
Know DBMS basics - normalization, SQL queries
Prepare your projects well - be ready to explain code
Basic data structures and algorithms

rounds.name: HR Interview
rounds.description: Assesses communication, attitude, and cultural fit
tips: Be confident and maintain eye contact
Research TCS values and recent news
Prepare for 'Tell me about yourself' and 'Why TCS?'
Show willingness to relocate and work in any domain

overview: Infosys conducts a multi-stage selection process including online tests, technical interviews, and HR rounds. They emphasize logical thinking and communication skills.

rounds.name: InfyTQ / Online Assessment
rounds.description: Tests aptitude, logical reasoning, and programming skills
tips: Practice puzzle-solving and logical reasoning
Strong focus on programming fundamentals
Time management is crucial - questions are timed

rounds.name: Technical Interview
rounds.description: Deep dive into technical skills and projects
tips: Know at least one programming language thoroughly
Be prepared to write code on paper/whiteboard
Understand your projects end-to-end
DBMS and networking basics are commonly asked

rounds.name: HR Interview
rounds.description: Evaluates soft skills and company fit
tips: Research Infosys's recent projects and values
Prepare behavioral questions using STAR method
Show enthusiasm for learning and growth
Be honest about your strengths and weaknesses

commonQuestions: Why do you want to join Infosys?
Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

overview: Wipro's recruitment process includes online tests, technical interviews, and HR rounds. They value adaptability and problem-solving skills.

rounds.name: Wipro NLTH (National Level Talent Hunt)
rounds.description: Online test with aptitude, logical reasoning, and written communication
tips: Essay writing is important - practice clear, concise writing
Quantitative section is moderate difficulty
Logical reasoning needs practice

rounds.name: Technical Interview
rounds.description: Tests programming skills and CS fundamentals
tips: Focus on data structures and algorithms
Know OOPS concepts with real-world examples
Prepare to explain your resume projects in detail
Basic OS concepts like process, thread, memory

rounds.name: HR Interview
tips: Know Wipro's Spirit of values
Be clear about your career aspirations
Show flexibility for roles and locations
Demonstrate good communication skills

overview: Amazon has a rigorous interview process focused on Leadership Principles and technical excellence. Expect multiple rounds of behavioral and coding interviews.

rounds.name: Online Assessment (OA)
tips: Practice LeetCode medium/hard problems
Focus on arrays, strings, trees, graphs, DP
Work simulation tests Leadership Principles
Time yourself - usually 90-120 minutes

rounds.name: Phone Screen
rounds.description: 45-60 min coding interview with LP questions
tips: Think out loud while solving problems
Clarify requirements before coding
Practice explaining your approach clearly
Prepare 2-3 STAR stories for LP questions

rounds.name: Onsite/Virtual Loop
rounds.description: 4-5 rounds of coding + system design + LP
tips: Each round tests different LPs - prepare examples
System design: practice designing scalable systems
Coding: focus on optimal solutions with clean code
Bar Raiser round is crucial - be your authentic self

commonQuestions: Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager
Describe a situation where you had to make a decision with incomplete data
How would you scale a system to handle 1M requests/second?
